{{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 417: ''"Homesickness"''}} {{Class 2e}} {{Audio|file=D2 - Quiet dusk coming early - The Caretaker (youtube).mp3|volume=0.25|align=center|data-options=",loop"}} '''Level 417''' is the 418th level of the Backrooms.
[[File:Cloudy Backrooms.jpg|Level 570 1.jpg]] The Outside
=='''—Description—'''== '''Level 417''' is divided into two parts: '''The Outside''' and '''The Inside'''. The Outside appears as a maze of cobbled streets set amidst a series of ruined, abandoned and moldy buildings, joint side by side. You can come across all kinds of buildings, but they are usually European. There is no day-night cycle, in fact it is always day, but the sky is constantly cloudy, so you can't see the sun. The streets have a perennial layer of dense fog that starts from the floor and can rise from the pelvis to the head. This part of the level is quite peaceful, as it is inhabited only by one entity: '''The Golem'''. This entity randomly roams The Outside (few specimens of it have been spotted) and it is neutral, meaning it only becomes aggressive if you bother it. Occasionally, you could come across variations of the level, for example a road covered with vegetation, snow, etc. or a narrower/wider street.
[[File:Variant Outside.jpg]] Variant of The Outside
When you find a door in one of the buildings that is not locked or broken, and you enter it, you can reach the other part of the level: The Inside. This place does not correspond to the structure that the building shows from the outside. It looks like the interior of a normal vintage house, but darker than normal, with only a few bulbs emitting a dim warm light. The rooms are totally random: they can range from large rooms to small rooms; from well-decorated rooms to completely empty rooms; from living room to bathroom to basement to attic consecutively, without necessarily going up or down the stairs (or maybe the basement is up the stairs and the attic down the stairs); from rooms in a certain style and color to rooms in a completely different and contrasting look (always vintage). In summary, they have no logic at all. Windows are very rare here, but if there were, nothing would be seen outside of them due to the amount of fog present. Moreover, in the rooms you can find [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and other useful resources. However, one thing that all the rooms have in common and makes The Inside dangerous is the constant feeling of '''nostalgia''', which grows as you stay in the level, causing an effect similar to that of [[Level 18]] or other analogous levels. As the nostalgia increases, it will cause you to hallucinate and you will see something that causes you even more nostalgia, for example your parents, grandmother, pet, etc. They will act in a very normal way, to fool you even more and convince you to stay in The Inside. The problem is that if you stay in there for too long, without finding an exit, then you will '''[REDACTED]'''! =='''—Entities—'''==
[[File:Golem.jpg]] A drawing of every possible stage of the Golem
This level has only one entity: The Golem. It is typically made of stone and has a humanoid shape, plus it has blue lights in its eyes and between the cracks in the rocks it is made of. It is neutral and it wanders undisturbed around The Outside or stands still like a statue in random spots and if you look at it for too long, touch it or disturb it in any way, it can become very aggressive and dangerous, turning the lights from blue to red. It is speculated that sometimes you may come across a rare good specimen, with green lights, that helps you and does not turn bad. In The Inside, no entity has been sighted, in fact in that place the main hazard is nostalgia. However, it is still unclear whether the other living things sighted, such as relatives or pets of the wanderers, are just hallucinations provoked by nostalgia or shape-shifting entities. =='''—Colonies and Outposts—'''==
[[File:Inside Entrance.jpg]] An entrance to The Inside
==='''Friends of the Golems'''=== *This is the only outpost, and it is only made up of about fifteen people. *They are open to trade and can help you, but only if you show interest in golems and worship them. *If they see you bothering a Golem then they will become very hostile. *''That's what friends do, isn't it?'' Given the nature of the level, colonies cannot exist in The Inside, but in The Outside passing MEGs occasionally build temporary camps, thanks to the trait of this level to be a junction for other levels. Only in serious cases, few MEGs go to The Inside to search useful resources. =='''—Entrances and exits—'''==
[[File:The Inside1.jpg]] Example of a room in The Inside (stairs)
==='''Entrances'''=== *If you get lost in the fog, no matter what level you are in, you have a small chance (about 10%) of landing in this level; *If you enter a vintage wardrobe and lock yourself inside in the dark, you may find yourself in this level after a while. The chances decrease a bit if it is a modern wardrobe; *If in [[Level 0]], or any other level, you find a wall with no wallpaper, showing moldy bricks, noclip through it may take you to this level. The possibility increases a lot if you are in the middle of the fog; *If in any level with houses, like [[Level 9]], you enter one of them and feel homesick, you will probably be taken to this level; *If in [[The Hub]] or in [[Level 21]] you find a door with the number 417 written on it and enter it, you will end up in this level; *Climbing a tree or wandering in certain foggy areas in [[Level 416]], and getting lost in the fog, has a very high chance to bring you to this level. ==='''Exits'''===
[[File:The Inside2.jpg]] Another room in The Inside (kitchen)
*The main exit to this level is wandering in the fog until you find yourself in a different foggy level; *If in The Inside you find a door in the same style of [[Level 5]], and you enter it, it will lead you to that level; *Getting lost in the vegetation in a variant of The Outside could lead you, with low probability, to any level with forests and lots of vegetation; *On very rare occasions the sky may become dark. In that case, getting lost in the fog has a good chance of taking you to [[Level 9]] or, with less chance, to another random dark level; *If you find a snowy variant of The Outside, getting lost in the fog or lying in the snow for a while could take you to any snowy level, like the nearby [[Level 415]] or the distant [[Level 998]]; *In The Inside, noclip through a wall with the same wallpaper as [[Level 0]], will take you to that level. It could also work with the walls of other levels, but it is very rare; *Being killed by a Golem has a small chance to take you to a graveyard level like [[Level 207]]. *Destroying and falling or noclip through the floor in The Outside can get you to [[The Hub]] (35%), to [[The Void]] (40%) or to [[Level 8]] (25%). ''The percentages have been approximately estimated from the MEG''; *Destroying and falling or noclip through the floor in The Inside, on the other hand, could get you into [[Level 6]], as well as into [[The Void]] (fifty fifty); *Noclip through a fridge or a kitchen counter can take you to [[Level 610]]; *There is an undiscovered way to get from this level to the next, the experts are working on it... ==—Addendum—==

The following words are classified as ''Top Secret'' and fall under the jurisdiction of the MEG. The text was discovered on a sheet of yellowed paper, recovered on 06/03/2██ by a MEG operative within the lower depths of ''The Inside''. It is presumed to be a diary page belonging to a wanderer, written during his final moments of life. Despite extensive searches in the surrounding area, no corpse — nor any additional pages — were ever found.
Date: December 23rd, ███ – Time: ??? I miss you, Mom. Yesterday, the watch you gave me broke. Since then, I’ve been trembling with fear… it feels as though time itself has stopped. I feel lost, my dear sister. I miss hearing your playful screams. The ones I hear here sound different… far too different. I think years have passed… or days… or perhaps mere moments since I lost myself in this strange place. And yet, sometimes, I still sense the smell of Mom’s lasagna. Each night, before I sleep, I also hear the meowing of our sweet Mr Mist — or at least, I believe it’s him. But here, the mist isn’t so… kind. Still, do you know what? I feel better now. Truly. Sadness no longer bites — it caresses. And nostalgia wraps around me gently, lulling me. Tomorrow should be Christmas Eve, if time still matters for anything. I miss our hearty family dinners on that special day — especially those from when Dad was still alive. I miss hearing him laugh at the table and watching him doze off with a glass of whisky in his hand. Dad… perhaps you were right. Perhaps a God truly exists. This place is so ██████. Only a God could have made it. But this isn’t Heaven, I know that well. If it were, I would have seen you again. Well… I have seen you, actually. Hundreds of times. You were kind, affectionate, even more than I remembered. But that wasn’t you. None of you were. I wished you were… At first, I didn’t understand. You all laughed and spoke with me. Then I saw the eyes. The eyes never lie, Dad. They never do. Maybe it’s right this way. Maybe it’s time… to accept that this is my home now. And the shadows that speak to me… perhaps they are my new family. Faded memories growing sharper, more vivid. No — not memories. '''''They''''' have a will of their own. But I’m afraid, Dad. Time no longer moves, and neither do I. I cannot remember how long I’ve been sitting on this old wooden chair. Motionless. As I write, I feel my movements growing slower and heavier. I feel my muscles stiffen, as if they were turning to stone. I hope Mom is still well, little sis. I hope you both live a long life, before joining Dad. Before joining me. But I’m not sad. I swear I’m not. I’m happy. Happy not to die alone. After all, you’re all here, beside me. Very, very close. Ve ver y c clo cl os e c l se
